_comment: Anthropic Messages API tool definitions for FridgeForage. Pass these in the `tools` array. Force a mode by setting tool_choice = { type: 'tool', name: 'submit_inventory_intake' } (or submit_recipe). The input_schema is what makes malformed JSON structurally impossible — enums and types are validated by the API before the tool_use block is returned. The client MUST still re-validate (see app/validation.ts): an enum can only be one of the allowed values, but the model can still pick the wrong allowed value, hallucinate a shelf life, or omit a field.

tools.name: submit_inventory_intake
tools.description: Submit the food items extracted and normalized from a receipt, photo, voice transcript, or typed list. Call exactly once. Discard non-food line items. Use conservative, shortest-safe shelf-life estimates.

processing_status.description: SUCCESS = items extracted cleanly. PARTIAL = some input was unreadable. EMPTY = no usable food items found.

normalized_name.description: Clean, human-readable food name, e.g. 'Organic Chicken Breast'.

quantity.description: Consumer count or weight as a number. Default 1.0 if not stated.

estimated_shelf_life_days.description: Shortest SAFE number of days before the item should be used or discarded. Respect the category ceilings in the system prompt. Round down when unsure.

barcode_gtin.description: GTIN/UPC only if literally present in the input. Otherwise null.

tools.name: submit_recipe
tools.description: Submit one fast, beginner-friendly recipe that consumes the expiring items the client provided. Call exactly once.

expiring_items_utilized.description: Subset of the item names the client sent.

mobile_ui_steps.description: One imperative action per step. Do not number inside the string.
